title: Execution Theater vs. System Design
key_story: Clean deck, green dashboard, silence in the field
blurb: Why the deck looks green while the field goes silent. Most execution failures are not skill gaps; they are system gaps dressed up as progress theater.

title: The Real Cost of Friction
key_story: Carrier program stalled 18 months over ownership ambiguity
blurb: What an 18-month stalled carrier program taught us about the hidden cost of ambiguous ownership. Friction does not announce itself; it just quietly eats your timeline.

title: When Market Language Fails
key_story: Samsung CPO program vs. "certified" label degradation
blurb: When the words your industry uses stop meaning anything, your customers lose trust and your operations lose discipline. What "certified" really costs when anyone can claim it.

title: Repeated Clarity Under Stress
key_story: Samsung volume spike: slowed down to re-anchor constraints
blurb: How Samsung handled a volume spike by slowing down, not speeding up. Why repeating the constraints out loud beats pushing harder.

title: When the Situation Changes Faster Than the Syllabus
key_story: Playbook fluency vs. ambiguity; mental models over memorization
blurb: Why playbook fluency fails the moment the ground shifts. Building mental models that survive the situation, not the slide deck.

title: What Breaks at the Handoff
key_story: Role fog, trigger drift, recovery tax
blurb: Handoffs are where most reverse logistics programs silently fail. Role fog, trigger drift, and the recovery tax nobody sees until it is too late.

title: Coordination Debt
key_story: Near-contract lost after reorg wiped sponsor and account exec
blurb: How a reorg wiped out a sponsor and an account exec, and nearly killed a near-signed contract. The coordination debt no one tracks until it comes due.

title: Structural Amnesia
key_story: Sales team reusing broken case study; team that rewired its system
blurb: When your team keeps repeating a broken case study because nobody rewrote the script. How to build structural memory instead of individual heroics.

title: The Circularity Delta
key_story: White paper with Peter Evans; 10-15% enterprise recovery rates
blurb: The white paper with Dr. Peter Evans that quantified the gap: enterprise recovery rates at 10-15%, with the rest bleeding into write-offs. Why orchestration beats vendor stacking.

title: The 80/20 of System Design
key_story: Note 7 crisis: battery as invisible critical constraint
blurb: What the Note 7 crisis revealed about invisible critical constraints. Why 80% of your system design effort should focus on 20% of the path.

title: Your System Is Only as Strong as the Questions You Ask
key_story: Three marketplace failures for Samsung CPO
blurb: Three marketplace failures from the Samsung CPO program. The questions your team is not asking are costing you more than the ones they are.

title: When Circularity Fails
key_story: "Vendor orchestration mirage" vs. Recovery-as-a-System
blurb: Why the "vendor orchestration mirage" collapses under real volume. Recovery-as-a-System as the alternative.

title: Execution Black Holes
key_story: TXU customer retention: 25% churn in 5-week window, cut to 3 weeks
blurb: How TXU cut a 5-week churn window to 3 weeks and saved 25% of retention dollars. Finding and closing the execution black holes nobody maps.

title: The Forbidden Low-Hanging Fruit
key_story: TXU: $36M annual value from testing the "optimized" sacred process
blurb: $36M in annual value left on the table because nobody would touch the "optimized" sacred process. Why the untouchable process is usually where the money is.

title: The CFO Filter
key_story: Finance kills programs that can't prove predictable contribution margin
blurb: Why Finance kills reverse logistics programs that cannot prove predictable contribution margin. The filter you need to pass before the budget conversation.

title: Returns Don't Have to Bleed Margin
key_story: Returns as recovery, not write-off; TICE introduction
blurb: Returns are treated as write-offs by default; they do not have to be. The recovery-first framing that changes the P&L math, and the introduction to TICE.

title: The Real KPI: Time-Discounted Net Recovery
key_story: 1%/week decay, capital turns math, "the clock is the leak"
blurb: Why your real KPI is time-discounted net recovery, not gross recovery. The 1%-per-week decay math and why the clock is the leak.

title: The Clock Is Still the Leak (and Sometimes the Fuse)
key_story: BCG toy trucks; Samsung vendor brittleness; trust premium
blurb: BCG's toy trucks, Samsung's brittle vendor stack, and the trust premium you earn by delivering on time. When the clock stops being a leak and starts being a fuse.

title: Overdesigned, Underperforming
key_story: $400K integration; Saint-Exupery quote
blurb: A $400K integration that made everything worse. Why over-designed systems accumulate assumption debt you cannot see until they fail.

title: Execution Leverage
key_story: Champion vs. challenger drift; clarity over control
blurb: The drift between your champion and the challenger running the account. Why clarity beats control when you cannot be in every meeting.

title: Not Broken. Just Misdesigned.
key_story: Samsung zero-inventory rule; solving the wrong problem efficiently
blurb: Samsung's zero-inventory rule looked elegant on the whiteboard. How "solving the wrong problem efficiently" is the most expensive form of execution.

title: Hand-Off Without Breakdown
key_story: 18-month carrier program; won next client on reference call
blurb: How an 18-month carrier program closed clean and won the next client on the reference call. The hand-off discipline that compounds into pipeline.

title: The Friction That Never Generates an Escalation
key_story: TXU decade-long useless report; four-team green dashboards with hemorrhaging cash
blurb: A decade-long useless report at TXU. Four teams reporting green dashboards while cash hemorrhaged. The friction that never escalates is the friction you lose to.

title: The Trade-In Ownership Mirage
key_story: Carrier-store clerk volunteers ownership; 8-10 sliver owners; old matrix org / one decision spine
blurb: A clerk at a carrier counter said he owned the trade-in program. So did seven other people, up to the CFO. Why every level of the org chart can name an owner, and none of them can move the program.

title: ITAD Has Always Been Bigger. The Mobile Playbook Is Moving In.
key_story: Samsung pricing-curve + champion-challenger; senior ITAD peer reports ~1/3 of current contacts came from phone industry
blurb: The smaller side of device recovery industrialized faster than the bigger one because of three structural pressures ITAD never had. Now mobile's talent is crossing over with the playbook in hand. What an ITAD shop should do this quarter.
